title: Merchant Roads
logline: The most important negotiation of the war was conducted over trading ledgers, not at a peace table.
description: Mira Solhand travels from Port Maren to Solmark to Drenford and ultimately to Vareth, carrying messages no official courier can carry. She moves through a war as a merchant — invisible, essential, and thoroughly aware of what she is doing.

title: The First Winter
logline: A young lord inherits a siege on the third day of his rule, with his father's body still warm.
description: The story of Vareth's forty-day siege as experienced by Aran Voss — not the strategic decisions but the weight of them. A governor who reads philosophy trying to keep 40,000 people alive through a winter when the food stores were set for 28,000.

title: The Last Road
logline: The roads that built the Compact are still there. So is the woman who knew them best.
description: A story set in the early Post-Collapse, following the old merchant routes between Drenford, Solmark, and Port Maren. The roads are intact but the agreements they ran on are not. Someone is still trying to hold the trade together from Drenford's market square.

title: The Silence After
logline: The first person to see Ashveil after the burning never told anyone what they found.
description: A traveler arrives at Ashveil three days after the burning, before the official record-keepers, before the soldiers. What they find — and choose not to report — reshapes everything that follows. A story told entirely from outside the political narratives.
